title: Xautoを呼び出して
summary_text: **Prioritized Findings** - **P0: Production workflow defaults still fall back to `assist=claude`, which mismatches the current `/vote`-first design baseline (`assist=none`).** Impact: if `FUGUE_ASSIST_ORCHESTRATOR_PROVIDER` is unset, routing resolves to Claude-assist by default, which can reintroduce Claude-dependent behavior and reduce autonomous reliability in Claude-constrained environments. Refs: [fugue-task-router.yml#L351](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-task-router.yml#L351), [fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L193](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L193), [fugue-tutti-router.yml#L1450](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-router.yml#L1450), contrasted with resolver default [resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L79](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L79). - **P1: `/vote` runtime floor is weaker than Kernel minimum (2 lanes vs 6), and phase-gate does not enforce a lane-count floor.** Impact: `/vote`-first autonomous execution can proceed with materially lower parallel diversity than Kernel doctrine expects. Refs: [/vote prompt floor at vote.md#L31](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/vote.md#L31), [Kernel minimum at kernel.md#L60](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L60), [phase checks enforce provider evidence but no lane-count check at kernel-phase-gate.sh#L310](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-phase-gate.sh#L310). - **P1: `/vote` launcher path lacks explicit run lifecycle guard/resume semantics present in `kernel` path; run continuity can degrade when `KERNEL_RUN_ID` is absent.** Impact: autonomous `/vote` sessions may rely on external launcher behavior for run identity; phase-gate falls back to `unknown-run` when run id is missing, which can blur provenance/continuity. Refs: [vote launcher direct pass-through at launchers/vote#L7](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/vote#L7), [kernel guarded lifecycle at launchers/kernel#L38](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L38), [phase-gate unknown-run fallback at kernel-phase-gate.sh#L15](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-phase-gate.sh#L15), and limited launcher coverage [test-vote-launchers.sh#L30](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-vote-launchers.sh#L30). - **P2 (coverage gap): Tests are biased to `assist=none` unit env, so production fallback drift (`'claude'`) is not caught early.** Impact: CI can pass while production workflow defaults remain misaligned. Refs: [test-route-task-handoff.sh#L89](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-route-task-handoff.sh#L89), [test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L88](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L88), and Claude-centric parity baseline [check-agent-matrix-parity.sh#L54](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-agent-matrix-parity.sh#L54).
